rulings.Case Title: ALBERT L. BOASBERG VS. ARAG INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltfs Complaint For Fraud And Punitive Damages, Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 15, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT ARAG INSURANCE COMPANY Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Complaint for Fraud and Punitive Damages. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend as to punitive damages claim. Plaintiff must plead facts that support malice, fraud, or oppression. Motion to strike denied as to remaining issues. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT L. BOASBERG VS. ARAG INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Demurrer, Demurrer, And Memorandum Ofp/A In Support Thereof Defts Demurrer;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 15, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT ARAG INSURANCE COMPANY Notice Of Hearing Re: Demurrer. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and second cause of action for common counts. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to third cause of action. Plaintiff to allege the names of the persons who made the fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KOKO FUJITA FLOWERS VS. DAVID SEAN GILLIMORE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 15, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT NATIONWIDE LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to first cause of action (failure to follow instructions), fourth cause of action (unsuitability), fifth cause of action (fraud), ninth cause of action (CLRA), and tenth cause of action (violation of CC 3372) as conceded. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to second and seventh causes of action (breach of fiduciary duty) because plaintiff cannot allege the existence of a fiduciary duty. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to eighth cause of action (violation of corporations code) because cited statute involves misrepresentations regarding securities and this case does not involve securities under Corp. Code section 25019. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to eleventh, thirteen and fourteenth causes of action to plead a claim for rescission. Plaintiff granted leave to attempt to plead a negligence cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KOKO FUJITA FLOWERS VS. DAVID SEAN GILLIMORE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 15, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT JOHN HANCOCK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY (U.S.A.) DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to first cause of action (failure to follow instructions), fourth cause of action (unsuitability), fifth cause of action (fraud), and ninth cause of action (CLRA) as conceded. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to second and seventh causes of action (breach of fiduciary duty) because plaintiff cannot allege the existence of a fiduciary duty. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to eighth cause of action (violation of corporations code) because cited statute involves mis-representations regarding securities and this case does not involve securities under Corp. Code section 25019. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the thirteenth cause of action to plead a claim for rescission. Plaintiff granted leave to attempt to plead a negligence cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHIKO MASUDA VS. HIDETO MATSUSHIMA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 15, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT HIDETO MATSUSHIMA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must specify why she did not learn of defendant's identity earlier. There is no showing of diligence why plaintiff did not attempt to discover defendant's true name in 2007-2008. =(302/CWW)
